commit.message:
Fix cipher suite typo: POLY1205 -> POLY1305 in TLS docs (#5879)

`ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1205` was listed as a valid cipher suite in
three SSL/TLS documentation pages — the correct name is
`ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305`. Copying the incorrect value results in
`SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list:no cipher match` errors.

## Changes

- **`solutions/observability/apm/apm-server/ssl-tls-input-settings.md`**
— corrected cipher name
-
**`solutions/observability/apm/apm-server/ssl-tls-output-settings.md`**
— corrected cipher name
- **`reference/fleet/elastic-agent-ssl-configuration.md`** — corrected
cipher name

All other cipher suite names in these files were verified against the Go
`crypto/tls` source and are correct.

Part of https://github.com/elastic/apm-server/issues/20876

---------

commit.message:
Move PHI/HIPAA ownership from Security Guardian to Privacy Guardian

Security Guardian: removed PHI-specific sections (Refinement Checklist,
Threat Modeling, Data Protection, Logging). Now defers to Privacy Guardian
for PHI/HIPAA with pointer references instead of duplicating rules.

Dev Guardian: pre-compliance checklist simplified — flags PHI for
Privacy Guardian review instead of checking HIPAA rules itself.

PO Guardian: Step 5b now consults Privacy Guardian alongside Security,
Platform, and Delivery during specification.

Global Baseline: rule #4 slimmed to PII focus, points to Privacy
Guardian for PHI/HIPAA.

Privacy Guardian (PR #77) provides comprehensive coverage:
3-tier data classification, 18 HIPAA identifiers, GDPR/CCPA/NIST,
substance abuse (42 CFR Part 2), mental health, genetic/biometric,
quasi-identifiers, and latent privacy risk detection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: global ScanStore as single source of truth

ScanStore now holds detailed results for all modules (not just
summaries). Overview scan stores full data; module VMs load from
the store instantly via loadFromStore() instead of re-scanning.

Architecture:
- ScanStore: cleanResults, threats, systemInfo, updates,
  duplicateGroups, largeFiles, similarImages, tempFiles
- SmartCareVM: stores full results from each concurrent scan task
- Module VMs: loadFromStore() reads pre-existing data on appear;
  own scans write back to the store
- Views: .task calls loadFromStore() — instant results if Overview
  has already run, otherwise shows empty state as before

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service — KG-backed urgency + resolution (ENG-03) (#143)

* feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service (ENG-03)

Implement ForeshadowingTracker — a backend service that queries KG entities
of type 'foreshadowing' and provides urgency-sorted listing + resolution
capability.

Key decisions:
- Pure SQLite queries (INV-4: Memory-First, zero LLM calls)
- resolve() is a structured data operation (attributes_json flag toggle),
  NOT a Skill invocation (INV-6 compliant)
- Deterministic clock injection (P4) — no Date.now() in logic
- 30s TTL cache consistent with storyStatusService
- Graceful degradation: returns [] until kg_entities CHECK constraint is
  extended to include 'foreshadowing' type (migration 0013 gap)
- Imports DbLike/DbStatement from storyStatusService (shared DB abstraction)

Test coverage: 37 tests using in-memory SQLite (real SQL queries, not mocks):
- listActive: empty project, single/multiple items, urgency sort, resolved
  exclusion, project isolation, type filtering
- resolve: success, non-existent, wrong project, already-resolved, attribute
  preservation, cache auto-invalidation
- openDays/urgency: exact days, partial days, today
- firstChapterHint: present, absent, non-string, empty attributes
- Cache: TTL (30s), boundary (29999ms vs 30000ms), invalidation, per-project
- Dispose: listActive throws, resolve throws, cache cleared

Closes #142

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engagement): align foreshadowing tracker with KG schema + spec urgency model

R1 audit findings addressed:
- ISO timestamp parsing: created_at is TEXT in production (kgCoreService
  writes new Date().toISOString()), now parsed via new Date(str).getTime()
- resolve() writes ISO string for updated_at (matches KG convention)
- urgency field: clamped to [0, 1] range via min(1.0, openDays / 30),
  with @deviation JSDoc documenting chapter-based spec vs time-based impl
- Test schema: created_at/updated_at changed from INTEGER to TEXT to match
  production kg_entities schema
- URGENCY_DECAY_DAYS = 30 ≈ 10 chapters at ~3 days/chapter writing pace

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use PkValuesEqual in RowMatchesPrimaryKey; soften CHANGELOG wording

- Fix RowMatchesPrimaryKey to use PkValuesEqual (case-insensitive,
  Guid/decimal tolerant) instead of strict string equality, matching
  the comparison logic used by ALGet. Previously a Code-field PK
  differing only by case would not be caught as a duplicate by Insert.
- Soften CHANGELOG entry to acknowledge that symbol-only tables with
  composite PKs that don't include field 1 may still diverge from BC.

fix: revise coding archetype states for proper SDLC coverage

Updated coding archetype lifecycle to reflect actual PR-based workflow:
- Added explicit PR states: pr-open, pr-review, pr-approved, merged
- Removed generic 'reviewing' and 'deploying' states
- New flow: preparing → implementing → testing → pr-open → pr-review → pr-approved → merged → complete/failed

This provides better monitoring visibility for:
- When PRs are created and waiting for review
- When PRs are under active review
- When PRs are approved and ready to merge
- When PRs are actually merged

Updated in both the example progression (line 205) and design decisions (line 1068).

commit.message:
feat(vllm-ops): add skill with GB10+Gemma4 NVFP4 tuning experience

- Add vllm-ops skill with 4 reference files covering install,
  configuration, deployment, and performance tuning
- Add hardware-model-specific.md with hands-on tuning knowledge for
  NVIDIA GB10 (Grace Blackwell) + Gemma4-26B-A4B NVFP4:
  * NVFP4 weight loading bug fix (gemma4.py patch mount required)
  * Correct chat template (266-line model-native, not moe_tools.jinja)
  * Optimal params: max_num_seqs=24, max_num_batched_tokens=49152
  * gpu_memory_utilization via profiler (0.9018-0.9026 range)
  * cudagraph_capture_sizes=[1,2,4,8,16,24]
  * FlashInfer autotuner warm-up: wait 2-5 min before benchmarking
  * Measured sustained throughput: 415 tok/s (peak 600) at 24 concurrency
  * KV cache monitoring guide for concurrency tuning
  * Correct vllm bench serve invocation for offline environments

fix: detect empty GitHub releases and propagate exit code on failure (#57)

* chore: update DotnetPackaging packages to 10.1.7

Update all DotnetPackaging package references from 10.1.0 to 10.1.7.
Flatpak stays at 9.0.6 (removed from DotnetPackaging in v10.0.0).

Notable upstream changes in 10.1.1–10.1.7:
- Improved error handling in DotnetPublisher (better build error extraction)
- RPM packaging fixes (AppStream metadata, URI parsing, header structure)
- IsTerminal changed to Maybe<bool> (inferred from OutputType)
- systemd service/daemon support for .deb packages
- from-directory subcommand in CLI tool

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: detect empty GitHub releases and propagate exit code on failure

When all package builds fail, the deployer now:
- Deletes the empty release instead of leaving it on GitHub
- Cleans up the release if an exception occurs during upload
- Returns a non-zero exit code so CI pipelines fail correctly

The exit code bug was caused by SetAction(Func<ParseResult, Task>) which
ignores Environment.ExitCode. Now uses a closure variable returned from Main.
